zh.Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭藍納可爾卡瓦斯
zh.lifeSummary: William Brailey  Fraser於1212年出生在蘇格蘭。他於1248年之前與Catherine Troot在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1280年在他的家鄉享年68歲。

zh-hans.Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Scotland: 苏格兰藍納可爾卡瓦斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Brailey  Fraser于1212年出生在苏格兰。他于1248年之前与Catherine Troot在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1280年在他的家乡享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: Brailey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Brayley Barton in Devon, which is named from the Bray river (a back formation from High Bray, which is from Celtic bre ‘hill’ or Old English brǣg ‘brow’) + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

variants.name: Bralley
meanings: Probably a variant of   Brailey   or   Braley  .

variants.name: Braley
meanings: English: variant of   Brailey  .

nameMeanings.name: Fraser
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: apparently a nickname from Anglo-Norman French fraser(e), fresere ‘strawberry plant’. Fresel and Frisel, from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, are early variants of the Fraser surname, the modern Gaelicized form of which is Friseal. See Frizzell . The crest on the Scottish family's coat of arms takes the form of a strawberry plant, but its antiquity is unknown. Claims of a habitational derivation, in particular from a place called la Frézelière in Anjou (France), are attractive but they lack verifiable evidence.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

History: There are two clans Fraser in Scotland, with common ancestry going back to Simon Fraser of Keith in East Lothian, who lived in the 12th century. One of these clans has its seat at Philorth Castle (subsequently re-named Cairnbulg) on the northeast coast of Scotland. Sir Alexander Fraser, 8th laird of Philorth (c. 1536–1623) converted the fishing village of Faithlie into the burgh of Fraserburgh in the 1590s. The other clan is Fraser of Lovat, associated mainly with the city of Inverness are. They are descended from Simon Fraser, a younger son, who lived in the early 14th century. In Gaelic, the head of Clan Fraser of Lovat is known as Mac Shimi ‘son of Simon’.

variants.name: Frayser
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frasier
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frasure
meanings: Altered form of Scottish   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frazer
meanings: Variant of   Fraser  , associated chiefly with northern Ireland.

variants.name: Fraizer
meanings: Altered form of Scottish   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frashier
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Fraiser
meanings: Altered form of Scottish   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frizzell
meanings: English and Irish: nickname from Old French fresel, frisel ‘lace, ribbon, braid, brooch, frill, ruff’, for someone with an ornate style of dress, or ‘object of little value’ (a diminutive of frese, of ancient Germanic origin).
Scottish: from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, an alternative form of Fraser .

variants.name: Frazier
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Fraser  .
Americanized form of French Frégeau (see Fregeau ).

variants.name: Frasher
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .
Possibly an altered form of German Fratzscher (see Frutchey ).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William Brailey  Fraser was born in 1212, in Scotland. He married Catherine Troot before 1248, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1280, in his hometown, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: William Brailey  Fraser est né en 1212, à Écosse. Il a épousé Catherine Troot avant 1248, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1280, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: William Brailey  Fraser wurde 1212 in Scotland geboren. Er hat vor 1248 in Scotland Catherine Troot geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1280 im Alter von 68 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: William Brailey  Fraser è nato il nel 1212 nel Scotland. Lui sposato Catherine Troot prima del 1248, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1280, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 68 anni.

ja.Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Scotland: スコットランドラナルクシュアイアカーンワス
ja.lifeSummary: William Brailey  Fraserは1212年にスコットランドで生まれた。1248年以前にスコットランドでCatherine Trootと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1280年に故郷で68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Brailey  Fraser(은)는 1212년 스컷랜드에서 태어났다. 그는 1248년 이전 스컷랜드에서 Catherine Troot(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1280년 그의 고향에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Brailey  Fraser nasceu em 1212, em Scotland. Ele casou-se com Catherine Troot antes 1248, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1280, em sua cidade natal, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: William Brailey  Fraser родился в 1212 г. в местности Шотландия. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine Troot перед 1248 г. в местности Шотландия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1280 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 68 лет.

es.lifeSummary: William Brailey  Fraser nació en 1212, en Scotland. Se casó con Catherine Troot antes de 1248, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1280, en su pueblo, a la edad de 68 años.
